items.definition_en: An IP address is a unique number for devices on a network.
items.example_en: You need an IP address to connect your computer online.

items.definition_en: A DNS server translates website names into IP addresses.
items.example_en: If the DNS server is down, websites might not load.

items.definition_en: A fiber optic cable transmits data as light for fast internet.
items.example_en: My new apartment uses a fiber optic cable for internet.

items.definition_en: A network adapter connects a device to a network.
items.example_en: My laptop has a wireless network adapter built in.

items.definition_en: An access point lets devices connect to a wireless network.
items.example_en: The office has an access point on every floor.

items.definition_en: An ethernet port is a socket for wired network cables.
items.example_en: Plug the cable into the ethernet port for a stable connection.

items.definition_en: An uplink is a connection from a device to a higher network.
items.example_en: We improved our internet speed by upgrading the uplink.

items.definition_en: A load balancer distributes internet traffic across servers.
items.example_en: The website uses a load balancer to handle many users.

items.definition_en: A proxy server acts as an intermediary for requests from clients.
items.example_en: A proxy server helps hide your real IP address online.

items.definition_en: A network socket connects devices to a local wired network.
items.example_en: The hotel room has a network socket near the desk.

items.definition_en: A range extender increases the coverage of a wireless network.
items.example_en: We installed a range extender to get WiFi in the garden.

items.definition_en: A packet is a small piece of data sent over a network.
items.example_en: Data travels over the internet in the form of packets.

items.definition_en: An encryption key is a secret code for securing transmitted data.
items.example_en: Never share your WiFi encryption key with strangers.

items.definition_en: A subnet is a smaller part of a larger network.
items.example_en: Each department uses a separate subnet for security.

items.definition_en: A gateway connects different networks and directs traffic between them.
items.example_en: All our email runs through the company’s gateway.

items.definition_en: A data center houses many servers for internet services.
items.example_en: Big companies store their websites in a data center.

items.definition_en: A MAC address is a unique identifier for a network device.
items.example_en: You can block devices by their MAC address.

items.definition_en: A static IP is a fixed address for a device on a network.
items.example_en: Businesses often prefer a static IP for stability.

items.definition_en: An SSL certificate secures information sent between a website and users.
items.example_en: Websites need an SSL certificate to show the padlock icon.

items.definition_en: An ARP table stores IP and MAC address pairs for a network.
items.example_en: The router uses the ARP table to find devices on the network.
